stemHtml: When descending for an airfield situated in a valley and encountering strong winds blowing perpendicular to the mountain range surrounding the valley (referred to as "Foehn conditions"), what risks need to be accounted for during the descent and final approach?

options.a: While having good visibility and strong winds at mountain top level, visibility will be strongly reduced by fog in turbulent air close to the ground.
options.b: Lee waves may develop overhead the valley, with severe turbulence at mountain top level and smooth air close to the ground.
options.c: Rotor zones may develop at the mountain top level, and lee waves with strong clear air turbulence within the valley.
options.d: Rotor zones may develop with severe turbulence due to opposite, strong winds at mountain tops and ground level.
